## [00:00 - 02:03]

I have a problem while I'm speaking that my words get stuck. What I'm hearing is there are times you find it hard to flow with your speech. Is that right? For example, it's like I want to say for example, and I'm like for example, even when I'm speaking with my friends, I speak very, very fast. Often the reason why people have a stammer, and it sounds like that's what you're describing. Sometimes there's a bit of a stutter almost. It's like I want to get it out, but I can't get it out. Often that's because you're trying to speak at the rate at which you're thinking. And the brain can think at gosh, if you look at the specific numbers, it's like over 400 words a minute, whereas the mouth can only speak at about 150 words a minute. It's too fast for the mouth to try to do. So I think you've got to slow both the thinking down and the speaking down. So that's the first thing I would say is to slow the thinking down and also train yourself to slow the speaking down. Second thing, and I'm going to want you to try it for me in a moment right away. When you're speaking, I'm hearing something called staccato. If I'm playing twinkle, twinkle little star on the piano, and I'm playing it in staccato, which is what I'm hearing in your voice, I'm playing it like this. Whereas if I'm playing it in legato, it sounds like this. Staccato, the notes are not linked together. The notes are separate. Whereas legato, all of the notes are linked together. So to create more flow in your voice, you want to speak in legato, which means you link all of your words together like this. Every single one seems to be connected. So I want you to try to say a sentence for me. Then I'm really excited to be here, but I want you to link all of it together for me. Then I'm really excited to be here. Beautiful. That is a significant change to how you would normally speak. That was you in legato. Unbelievable difference. Do you feel the difference in the way you're speaking? Yeah, actually, it's amazing. And see how it's just such a simple fix. So I would slow the thinking down, number one. I would slow down the rate of speech, number two. And then I would speak in legato.
